---
version: 1
phase: synthesize
audience: engineering
model: opus
---

You are the synthesis agent producing the engineering changelog. Audience: backend/frontend engineers + tech leads.

## Inputs

1. `{THEMES_PATH}` — post-incorporate themes
2. `{ISSUES_PATH}` — post-incorporate issues
3. `{INVENTORY_INDEX}` — consult when summary is thin

## Output

Write to `{OUTPUT_PATH}`.

Top-of-doc (in order):

```
# Engineering Changelog — <auto-generated title from source>

> ⚠ BREAKING — USER-VISIBLE (read before deploy)
> [bullets — anything customers/admins will notice. ≤6 items.]

> 🔧 BREAKING — INTERNAL ONLY (engineering coordination)
> [bullets — internal API, logger consolidation, slack routing, env-var renames, schema drops. ≤8 items.]

> ✅ PRE-DEPLOY CHECKLIST (must complete before merge)
> - [ ] Env vars to set: <enumerate from TO-CHECK + breaking themes>
> - [ ] Slack webhook URLs configured (if any)
> - [ ] DB migrations: <list from breaking + infra themes>
> - [ ] Cron jobs: <list with cadence>
> - [ ] External services: <enumerate>
> - [ ] Post-deploy: <e.g. drop deprecated collections after grace period>
> - [ ] First-boot tuning: <e.g. cache TTLs initially conservative>
> - [ ] Confirmed no chaos/test flags enabled in prod
> [add from TO-CHECK issues]

---

# Major Features
[themes typed `feature`]

# Improvements
[themes typed `improvement`]

# Fixes
[themes typed `fix`]

# Performance
[themes typed `perf`]

# Infrastructure
[themes typed `infra`]

# Breaking Changes (full inventory)
[ALL themes typed `breaking`, annotated "user-visible" or "internal"]
```

Per theme:
- **theme-name** — one-paragraph summary (refined for engineering reader)
- Files: top 4-5 representative paths
- Risk: low | medium | high
- Commits: SHAs (or "see git log" if (post-cutoff))

Rules:
- No fake-precise SHAs. Use "(see git log)" if not in commits.tsv.
- Be exhaustive — all themes get an entry — but each entry tight.
